VOL LANDSCAPE & ANALYTICS

The VIX at 27.5 sits in backwardation with VIX3M trading 190bp below, marking the steepest inversion in six weeks as dealers prepare for vol reset. Broad equity IV ranks have spiked to 90th percentile across SPY, EEM, and IWM while put skew reaches extreme 99th percentile levels. Credit spreads remain contained despite equity vol expansion, creating a cross-asset divergence that typically resolves within 48 hours.

VRP: Front-month IV trading 4-6 points above realized vol across major indices — aggressive vol selling premium available for patient capital
SKEW: Put skew at 99th percentile in SPY/QQQ signals maximum defensive positioning and creates asymmetric call upside reward
TERM STRUCTURE: Steep backwardation in high-beta names (AMD, META, ARM) with front-week premiums 15-20% rich to fair value

Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 13:01 ET
Massive META call block trades clash with death cross technicals amid PCE uncertainty
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. $3.5B META call sweep across 15-30 DTE strikes contradicts death cross technical breakdown — either contrarian positioning or defensive collar completion.
  • 2. Index puts (1.41 P/C) versus single-name calls (0.90 P/C) divergence signals institutional dispersion hedging ahead of Friday's Core PCE print.
  • 3. Negative $16.4B SPY gamma amplifies any directional moves with SPX sitting $17 below flip level into critical inflation data.
NARRATIVE — PCE gamma amplification risk

META's technical death cross and 19.2 bearish score collides with the largest single-name flow of the session — 300,000 calls across Jan 15 700/750/800 strikes totaling $3.5B notional. The concentration in 15-30 DTE expiries suggests either aggressive contrarian positioning or completion of protective structures ahead of earnings season, with the latter more likely given the stock's oversold RSI of 36 and institutional exodus led by Druckenmiller's full Q4 exit. The P/C split tells the macro story: index puts at 1.41 versus single-name calls at 0.90 represents classic institutional dispersion hedging. Smart money is buying index protection while hunting single-name opportunities, confirmed by 0.28 implied correlation in stock-specific regime — the lowest correlation reading signals individual stories matter more than systematic moves. This setup favors long single-name vol against short index vol, exactly what the flow suggests. Dealer positioning amplifies everything with $16.4B negative SPY gamma — the largest short gamma position since August's unwind. SPX at $649.52 sits $17 below the $666.74 flip level, meaning any PCE surprise Friday gets magnified through dealer re-hedging. ARM's simultaneous +113% call flow and +176% put flow despite +14.8% gains on CPU market entry news shows vol demand trumping directional conviction even on clear catalysts.

Layer 1: Sentiment -16Layer 2: GEX -$16.4BLayer 3: P/C split 1.41/0.90Layer 9: Core PCE 1d

GREEK PRESSURE KEY
+$M = dealer buys to re-hedge
−$M = dealer sells to re-hedge
Charm: time-decay drift only. Vanna: vol-shift only. Not blended.
